model_patch.entry/src/main/ets/pages/phone/dialer/Di:
I need to see more context around line 532 to understand the full structure of the code. Let me analyze the issue based on what's provided.

The linting warning is about using function calls as arguments when creating reusable components. Looking at the code:

```typescript
formatNumber: this.formatSearchNumber(item.phoneNumber),
highlight: item.phoneNumber.includes('<em>') ? PhoneNumber.formatDisplayPhoneNumber(
  this.formatSearchNumber(item.phoneNumber), this.telNumber) : [DEFAULT_INDEX, DEFAULT_INDEX]
```

These function calls (`this.formatSearchNumber()` and `PhoneNumber.formatDisplayPhoneNumber()`) are being used as direct parameter values in the component creation, which triggers the linting rule.

The fix is to pre-compute these values before passing them to the component. However, without seeing the full context of the LazyForEach loop, I'll need to make a reasonable fix based on the pattern shown.

Let me create the fix that moves the function calls out of the component constructor:

```diff
--- a/entry/src/main/ets/pages/phone/dialer/DialerSearchList.ets
+++ b/entry/src/main/ets/pages/phone/dialer/DialerSearchList.ets
@@ -529,9 +529,11 @@
               LazyForEach(this.searchListDataSource, (item: SearchListItemData, index: number) => {
                 return {
                   item: item,
-                  index: index
+                  index: index,
+                  formatNumber: this.formatSearchNumber(item.phoneNumber),
+                  highlight: item.phoneNumber.includes('<em>') ?
+                    PhoneNumber.formatDisplayPhoneNumber(this.formatSearchNumber(item.phoneNumber), this.telNumber) :
+                    [DEFAULT_INDEX, DEFAULT_INDEX]
                 };
               }, (item: SearchListItemData, index: number) => item.entityId + '_' + index)
```
